methods.description: Process for analyzing whole-rock samples for major and trace elements using X-ray fluorescence (XRF) and inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS).

steps.description: Whole-rock powders were weighed (0.7 g) and then mixed with 5.3 Li2B4O7, 0.4 g LiF and 0.3 g NH4NO3 in a 25 ml porcelain crucible.
steps.searchable_summary: Mix whole-rock powders with chemical reagents in a crucible

steps.description: The mixture powder was then transferred to a platinum alloy crucible, and 1 mL LiBr solution was added to the crucible and then dried.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer mixture to platinum crucible, add LiBr solution, and dry

steps.description: The sample was then melted in an automatic flame fusion machine and the resulting cooled glass was used for the X-ray fluorescence (XRF) major element analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: Melt sample in flame fusion machine and cool for XRF analysis

steps.description: For the trace element (and REE) analyses, whole-rock powders were weighed (50 mg) and then dissolved in 1 mL distilled HF and 0.5 mL HNO3 in 15 ml Savillex Teflon screw-cap capsules at 190 °C for 1 day, dried, and then digested with 0.5 mL HNO3 and dried again.
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve whole-rock powders in HF and HNO3, digest and dry

steps.description: The samples were then digested with 5 mL HNO3 and sealed at 130 °C for 3 hours. After cooling, the solutions were transferred to plastic bottles and diluted to 50 ml before analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest samples with HNO3, cool, transfer, and dilute for analysis

steps.description: The sample solutions were analyzed for trace elements via inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze sample solutions for trace elements using ICP-MS

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing the carbon and oxygen isotopic compositions of carbonates using the GasBench II method on a MAT253 mass spectrometer.

steps.description: Approximately 0.1 mg sample powder was added to a reaction bottle with a total volume of 12 ml.
steps.searchable_summary: Add sample powder to reaction bottle

steps.description: The sample powders were flushed for 600 s with pure He (99.999%) at a flow rate of 100 ml/min.
Each sample was then dissolved with 5 drops of 100% anhydrous H3PO4 at 72 °C for more than four hours to liberate CO2 from the calcite.
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve sample with H3PO4 to liberate CO2

steps.description: The CO2 was injected into a loop, separated via a chromatographic column (Poraplot Q, 25 m * 0.32 mm, Varian Ltd) at a constant 70 °C, dried using a Nafion membrane, and analyzed by an MAT 253 mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Inject CO2 into chromatographic column and analyze with mass spectrometer

methods.method_name: Sr, Nd, and Pb Isotopic Analysis
methods.description: Procedure for analyzing the Sr, Nd, and Pb isotope compositions using mass spectrometry and ion-exchange techniques.

steps.description: Rock chips of less than 20 mesh size were used. The sample powders were spiked with mixed isotope tracers and then dissolved in Teflon capsules with HF + HNO3.
steps.searchable_summary: Spike sample powders with isotope tracers and dissolve in HF + HNO3

steps.description: After the samples were fully dissolved and dried to remove HF, the samples were dissolved with HCl.
These samples were then dissolved with HClO4 and transferred to an HCl medium.
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve samples with HClO4 and transfer to HCl medium

steps.description: The Sr and REE fractions were separated in solution using cationic ion-exchangers (AG 50 W(H+), 200 to 400 mesh) in columns.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate Sr and REE fractions using ion-exchange columns

steps.description: Then, Sr and Nd were collected in the proper sequence via leaching with 4 mol ml−1 HCl.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect Sr and Nd via leaching with HCl

steps.description: The Sr fraction was purified again according to the above procedure for complete separation.
steps.searchable_summary: Purify Sr fraction for complete separation

steps.description: The collected Nd fraction was evaporated and dissolved in 0.2 mol ml−1 HCl, and the Nd was separated from the REE fractions using cationic ion-exchange columns and extraction and eluviation resin (HDEHP).
steps.searchable_summary: Evaporate and dissolve Nd fraction, separate using ion-exchange columns

steps.description: Sr isotopic measurements were performed on an MAT 262 mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Sr isotopic measurements using MAT 262 mass spectrometer

steps.description: The Nd isotopic measurement was performed on a Nu Plasma HRMC-ICP-MS (Nu Instruments) 262 mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Nd isotopic measurement using Nu Plasma HRMC-ICP-MS
